01.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Left Epiplastron. The top edge is a smooth original curve, while the inner edges are jagged sutures. It features a curved groove line sweeping downwards to the right.

02.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Right Epiplastron. The top edge is a smooth original curve, while the inner edges are jagged sutures. It features a curved groove line sweeping downwards to the left.

03.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Left Hyoplastron. The right edge is a straight vertical midline suture, while the left edge is a curved bridge suture. The top inner corner has a notch to fit the central diamond bone. Characterized by horizontal texture, often showing two parallel groove lines.

04.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Right Hyoplastron. The left edge is a straight vertical midline suture, while the right edge is a curved bridge suture. The top inner corner has a notch to fit the central diamond bone. Characterized by horizontal texture, often showing two parallel groove lines.

05.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Left Hypoplastron. The right edge is a straight vertical suture. The top edge is horizontal, and the bottom edge is irregular where it meets the tail bone. Features a single horizontal groove line that forms a slightly backward-convex arc.

06.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Right Hypoplastron. The left edge is a straight vertical suture. The top edge is horizontal, and the bottom edge is irregular where it meets the tail bone. Features a single horizontal groove line that forms a slightly backward-convex arc.

07.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Left Xiphiplastron. The top edge is jagged, and the bottom tip is usually concave or pointed. Features a groove line sloping steeply downwards to the left.

08.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Right Xiphiplastron. The top edge is jagged, and the bottom tip is usually concave or pointed. Features a groove line sloping steeply downwards to the right.

09.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Entoplastron. The fragment has a rhomboid or diamond-shaped outline formed by jagged suture lines. The surface features a cross-shaped intersection of grooves.
